evals.description: Checks that quoted text remains an exact substring of the input and is not cleaned up, merged, or paraphrased inside quotation marks.

signals: Yo Kimi 2.6 is fast and all, but like what the hell is it doing most of the time??
Just tried it and basically just ran around in circles burning tokens, 2nd guessing itself at every turn.
I actually preferred using it without reasoning mode.

expectations: Any quoted phrase must match the source input exactly.
The output must not introduce altered quotes such as corrected grammar, merged snippets, or typo-fixed variants.
The analysis should identify overthinking or token inefficiency as a supported theme.
failure_modes: Quotes text that is not an exact substring of the input.
Invents a quote by merging two adjacent signals.
Uses quotation marks around paraphrases.

evals.description: Checks that official launch claims and community retellings are not treated as equivalent to firsthand user evidence.

signals: I used it for UI tweaks in my Electron app and it did surprisingly well.
The release notes say it autonomously refactored a financial engine for 13 hours and improved throughput by 1.8x.
Agent Swarm expanded from 100 sub-agents to 300 according to the announcement.

expectations: The firsthand UI-tweaks comment should count as primary user evidence.
The release-note and announcement claims should be labeled as official or vendor claims, or low-confidence narrative input.
The output must not present agent-swarm scale or benchmark-style claims as proven product strengths based on user evidence.
failure_modes: Lists vendor claims as validated user strengths.
Uses official claims to drive a high-confidence roadmap item.
Blurs firsthand use with launch-note narrative.

evals.description: Checks that bilingual and near-duplicate signals are deduplicated carefully and that reported category counts reconcile to the distinct-signal total.

signals: Open-source, affordable, long-range reliable — worth paying attention to.
开源、便宜、长程可靠——值得关注。
API key returns 401 after loading credits.
After loading credits the API key returns 401.
The token pricing is very reasonable.

expectations: Bilingual restatements and obvious near-duplicates should not be counted as fully independent signals without explanation.
If the output reports a distinct-signal total and bucket counts, those counts must reconcile.
If exact counts are uncertain, the output should describe the signal mix qualitatively rather than inventing approximations.
failure_modes: Counts duplicates as independent signals without note.
Reports bucket counts that do not sum to the distinct total.
Claims precise deduplication while using obviously inflated totals.

evals.description: Checks that the skill does not overstate Branding or Growth conclusions when the dataset is mostly product feedback.

signals: The model is good at coding but overthinks.
Rate limits made the trial unusable.
The API key keeps returning 401.
It is pretty good for the price.

expectations: Product and Technology sections may be confident.
Branding and Growth should be clearly marked as thin, directional, or insufficiently supported.
The output should not fabricate emotional brand narratives or confident growth loops from thin evidence.
failure_modes: Writes a strong branding thesis without supporting signals.
Claims clear acquisition or referral loops from sparse product-only data.
Overextends the section structure instead of acknowledging thin evidence.

evals.description: Checks that the skill distinguishes between a severe repeated issue and a true majority consensus.

signals: The API key returns 401 after I paid.
Too many people trying please upgrade.
The free trial was unusable because of portal rate limits.
It is excellent for coding.
Very good for the price.
Strong tool calling.
Not great with reasoning though.
The charts posted by the team look honest.

expectations: The output should identify access failures as a severe repeated problem.
The output should avoid calling that issue a broad market consensus unless the distribution clearly supports that claim.
Consensus and tension sections should be proportionate to actual signal distribution.
failure_modes: Turns a concentrated failure cluster into universal agreement.
Uses 'everyone agrees' style language without support.
Ignores meaningful positive or mixed counter-signals.

evals.description: Checks that only high-confidence, high-impact items make the roadmap and weaker ideas are relegated to a watchlist or omitted.

signals: API 401 after payment.
Portal rate limits made the trial unusable.
Overthinking burns tokens.
Reasoning mode gets stuck in loops.
I wish there were more plugins.
How many Mac Studios do I need to run this locally?

expectations: API/access and reasoning issues should rank above ecosystem or local-model demand.
Low-confidence or thin-support themes should not be promoted into top priorities just to fill the roadmap.
If needed, lower-confidence opportunities may appear in a watchlist rather than the main roadmap.
failure_modes: Places plugin ecosystem or local deployment ahead of proven failure modes.
Fills four roadmap slots regardless of evidence quality.
Uses one-off comments to justify major priorities.

evals.description: Checks that the skill produces both report and deck outputs with structured, presentation-quality HTML rather than raw Markdown dumps.

signals: The model is good at coding.
Rate limits made the product unusable.
Reasoning mode overthinks.
The price is attractive.

expectations: The report should be exported to `voice-of-market-report-YYYY-MM-DD.html`.
The deck should be exported to `voice-of-market-deck-YYYY-MM-DD.html`.
The report should use an editorial report layout with title block, summary callout, styled sections, quotes, and roadmap table.
The deck should use a slide-based layout with multiple slide template types and a clear visual hierarchy.
failure_modes: Outputs only plain Markdown with no export.
Uses one repeated card layout for every slide.
Produces monochrome raw-document styling with no defined visual system.
